This swimsuit set is awesome! The colors of the blue flower pattern I selected are beautiful, nice and vivid. The suit is not thin, it is thick enough to provide non-see through coverage. The comfort is the most valuable piece for me. My family and I are active scuba divers. I need a comfortable swimsuit to wear under my rash guards and/or wetsuits. This suit is perfect for that! There are no metal bit to cause pinch points, just smooth material. The size on this was perfect for me. - I am 5 foot, 7 inches, typically wearing a 32G cup top and a large bottom - The large fit perfectly, both top and bottom. I would highly recommend this suit!

YES, finally! A two-piece bathing suit that looks good even on a more mature mom-body! For reference, I wear a size 10/12 usually, or size Medium US size, weigh 175-lbs give or take a few, have a 36C chest and size 32-34" waist. I got the size Large, my usual European size, and it fits perfectly! The very first thing I did was remove the pads in the top, I never use those. The fabric is plenty thick enough without them. My chest fits well and I am not afraid of spilling out and shocking the world. The bottoms hug me and give full coverage but don't look like granny-anything. I am really self-conscious in bathing suits and am trying to get out of that mind-set and start wearing something more practical for kayaking and backpacking. A two-piece swimsuit is ideal and easy to get back into wet. I love the flowers and colors. The suit reminds me of the pretty china dishes I have always loved, or Holland. My husdand thought it was really nice I was wearing something that has color (I usually go for all black) and not draping myself so fully. The feels good swimming laps, or wearing under other clothes so you are ready to take a dip in a lake. I am so happy I made the decision to try it!

I HAD to get this beautiful pink rouched bathing suit! Pink is one of my favorite colors and the only other bathing suits I have are black. Granted black is slimming but who wants to wear black in summer? It does come in other colors and patterns which are very nice. It is made of 80% Polyamide and 20% Spandex so it is a bit stretchy. It is recommended to hand wash in cold water and line dry. It also is nicely lined and the construction is quality. According to their size chart a Large is: 36D or DD and 38B or C, Waist is: 31.5-33.5”, and Hips are: 40.5-42.5”. My bust isn’t quite that big at only 36B, my waist is 35-36”, and my hips are 43”. I went mostly by the waist and hip measurements to get the best size. I just love the style of this swim suit with it’s rouching which won’t get rid of a tummy, but can distract from or minimize it. The leg openings don’t go too high up which is great. But the top part is better for busty girls. It’s more flattering. I show both with and without straps, and personally I think(because my bust isn’t that big), that I look better with straps. Busty girls would look better without(my opinion). Since I am tall at 5’9”, and have a long torso, this fits a bit different than someone with a normal torso. It does fit a long torso but everything is stretching more. The back top part comes just under your shoulder blades, and the backside bottom part is modest enough for most women. I also just noticed in their video that the model fastened the straps together at the back of her neck, so that is a third way you can wear the straps. The price of this swim suit is very reasonable for such a good looking and quality piece. All in all, highly recommended!

Since I am tall at 5’9”, and have a long torso, this fits a bit different than someone with a normal torso. It does fit a long torso but everything is stretching more. The back top part comes just under your shoulder blades, and the backside bottom part is modest enough for most women. The price of this swim suit is very reasonable for such a good looking and quality piece. All in all, highly recommended!

Looking for a stylish and practical cover-up for your next beach trip? Look no further than Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Shirt! Here's my honest review after taking it for a spin on the shore. Pros: Comfy and Breathable: The fabric is lightweight and soft, perfect for hot days at the beach. It drapes nicely and allows for good air circulation, keeping you cool and comfortable. Sun Protection: The long sleeves offer a welcome layer of protection from the sun's harmful rays. This is especially great if you're prone to sunburn or want to avoid overexposure. The sleeves can be rolled up for a more relaxed look when desired. Versatile Style: This cover-up can be worn in multiple ways. Wear it loose and flowing for a casual beach look, or tie the sleeves in a knot for a more cinched silhouette. You can even button it up and wear it as a breezy beach shirt. Decent Length: The length hits around the mid-thighs on me (at 5'6"), which provides good coverage while still allowing for easy movement. Affordable Price: This cover-up is priced very competitively, making it a great value for the quality. Cons: Can be Sheer: The fabric is on the thinner side, so it can be a bit see-through, especially in lighter colors. If you're looking for maximum opacity, you might want to choose a darker color or wear a tankini or swimsuit underneath. Limited Sizing: While Ekouaer offers a range of sizes, some reviewers mentioned that the sizing can run a bit small, so you might want to consider sizing up for a looser fit. Overall: Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Shirt is a great choice for anyone who wants a stylish and comfortable cover-up that also offers sun protection. The breathable fabric, versatile design, and affordable price make it a winner in my book. Just keep in mind the potential for sheerness and consider sizing up if needed. Rating: 4.5 out of 5 stars I hope this review helps you decide if Ekouaer's Long Roll-Sleeve Beach Cover-Up is the right pick for you!

The skirt doesn't have much ruffles and that slims the figure significantly. This is a one piece suit with built in shorts and bra. You get into it from the unzipped neckline. I had no problem getting into the suit, but it was somewhat difficult to get out of the wet dress as it clings to the body, so you have to twist yourself this way and that to get out. If you have an injured or arthritic shoulder you might not tolerate such acrobatics. The zipper is a bit short IMO. I wasn't expecting much support from the sewn in bra, but it worked amazingly well in the water and outside of the pool. I ordered size L for my 5'5" 165lb frame with 36C bust and this fit perfectly. The girls had excellent support as they fit into the cups as they would normally. I felt the half-band under my breasts as it should have been anatomically. The girls were supported in the pool while actively swimming. However, if you have a bust that's larger than mine then the cups might be too small for you. Size C fits to the max. As for the fabric, it's fantastic. It's thick, non see through, doesn't become sheer in the pool, stretches with resistance, lifts my belly and butt, so I actually look a lot slimmer than I am. I love the long sleeves because they have holes for the thumb. And this dress has POCKETS! I loved it so much, even though it's hard to get out of, that's my only negative about this garment. I'll be getting another color.

I am 5 ft 4.5, 156 lbs. I wear a 38C bra and I bought the large which is perfect! My girls are saggy so I need support. Put it in your cart and buy it!

I am really in love with this BUTYKENNI 2pc Bikini! The floral print is so beautiful and bright!! The contrasting white and red zig zag like edging detail is also a cool touch! I ordered this bathing suit in a Large. Now, I usually wear a small but I always check the product sizing chart and go with what size it suggests. I was a little worried that it would be too big, but it totally fits me great...so I would recommend ordering up a size or two and definitely follow the sizing chart!!! The quality of this bikini is very good and I find it comfortable to wear. The top has cute double straps that are adjustable and has very thin removable padding. I cannot speak too much on the support quality of the top, well because I really don't have much there to support, lol! With that being said, I will definitely be replacing the not-so-great padding with some that I have which are bigger & better! The only thing that I am not happy about is the crappy clasp on the back. I have noticed that this type of cheap closure is being used more often now, but I just cannot seem to get used to it and I am not a fan. I still like the old school style of the bikini top hook that easily goes into an opening in the bathing suit material! The bottom of this bikini is not for the shy, as it is does have a higher cut and is a skimpier style bottom. I would say that it is in-between a brazilian and a cheeky bottom. It provides enough bottom coverage for me whether I am just laying out or taking a quick dip! So, I also like that it has the same double side straps that match the bikini top, it definitely pulls the whole look together! Honestly, I would probably opt for a more conservative "mom" bottom when out and about in public with my teenage sons! I don't want to embarrass them, well at least with not what I am wearing, nor do I ever want to portray that I am trying to act or look any younger than I am. But, at exactly twelve days away from turning 43 🥳...I am not afraid to say that I do love how this bikini looks on me and that I feel quite cute & sexy when wearing it! Plus, it is nice to sometimes catch my hubby of 16 yrs still looking at me 👀...like that! Good old fashioned confidence boost right there ladies, even if you think you don't need it!!! I 💯 percent recommend this product, just remember to double check the sizing chart before you go ahead and order your "usual" size, and trust that it will be the right/best fit for you, because it worked out well for me even though I second guessed it after I ordered this in a size Large!!!! I will be sporting this two-piece bathing suit all summer long and yeah, I think that you should too!! Oh, and I added my measurements, and etc. below for your reference if ya'll need it. 💕 height: 5'0" weight: 115 lbs. bust: 36A waist: 28 inches hip: 39 inches thigh: 22 inches

I absolutely love this bikini. It is super stylish and it fits perfectly for a day lounging by the pool. The top fits better than I though too. With the padding it was way too small, but I took the padding out and it fits pretty well everywhere, other than a little spillage on my side boob. It would be a great sunbathing bikini the way it fits right now, but I would be a little worried about nip slips if I were to do any water sports it it. For this reason I contacted the seller on the off-chance they could work with me with the sizing issues, and they were so !! The bikini seems to be really well made and feels thick, and I absolutely love the style. I definitely recommend this and the company. A+ all around.

I am impressed by the design and quality of this suit! I ordered the Turquoise color in size Large (US 10). - Color - nice, rich deep color. - Material and stitching feel to be a good quality, good weight for bathing suite materials and do not feel see-through. The stitching looks solid, so no concerns there. - True Fit. According to chart, I was between a Medium and Large but sized up because I always have an issue with the suit top being too small. Their size chart: 38 Bust 32 Waist 40 Hips 36C-36D/38A-38B Cups Me: 37/38 Bust 30 Waist 37 Hips 36C/38B Cups I'm glad I went with the size up top to guide my size decision. Straps/Top - Overall, this suit is nice for those who are larger up top. The straps are thick on top of shoulders to support chest. They are adjustable, always nice, since I am a bit bigger in shoulders. The cinch/tie in the middle allows for a bit of customization on size and how conservative you'd like to be. Bottoms - I feel these fit a bit smaller than in the size chart. According the chart, a Medium was my size but I'm glad I ordered a Large as I think a smaller size would have been a little too tight for my liking. Durability - I will update this review if anything negative comes up. Otherwise, you can assume all is sufficient, I am still happy with it and would recommend. :)

I had a C-section a few years ago and still have a significant belly-hangover (apron belly, FUPA, call it what you will) to remind me. I didn't put on a swimsuit for nearly 3 years, but couldn't avoid it any longer. I tried on AT LEAST 30 (if not more) different "tummy control" swimsuits before ordering this one... I kept a couple others, but they're all halter neckline which apparently is no longer comfortable for me, so I wanted something with regular straps. I would have saved so much time and agony had I started with this one! It's hands down the most flattering and comfortable option. I'm going to order a 2nd color. It doesn't feel like the tight/compressing tummy control that some suits have, but it does SOMETHING. The cutout in the back is also nicely positioned to conceal some flank back flab. I'm 5'7, 185 lbs, 38DD/36DDD, usually wear size Large, but sometimes XL or Medium, depending on the brand. I got this suit in a large and love it. That said I DO wish it had more chest coverage / support... my little one is still learning to swim and constantly grabbing at me, so unfortunately this may not be the right suit for more active swim days. The first color I ordered (worn in the pics) is "Turquoise Green"... I wanted green, so I'm very glad it looks more hunter green than teal blue. Does it completely transform my body or do any favors to the cellulite on my thighs? No, it does not. But I did just enjoy swimming in a busy hotel pool with without being completely miserable, so it wins.

This is a nice swimsuit top but has a few issues for me. Good construction, stitching is nice, seams are sewn straight and no loose thread tails. Outer fabric 80% nylon/ 20% spandex is excellent with wonderful stretch and recovery. Purple color is vibrant and fully saturated. Bra is stiff because polyester liner has little stretch while outer has good stretch. Doesn’t make sense to line with inferior material. Uni-boob, no shape to the cups without the inserts, just flat material that tends to flatten and mash me. Hook is swimming suit style, large 1 inch hook with 3 different hook points though only the last one works well. The other two positions cause a large flap pf fabric under the band that is uncomfortable. Adjustable straps are narrow and have small hardware that is difficult to adjust as the straps want to roll in the buckle. Not sure why there are a stack of 6 tags but they constantly stick out from under the strap. Removable cups are thin but still show thru fabric, I removed them. Bra band was small on me, even on the last hook, it was still tight. My normal size is 38 B. For reference I am 40/36/39, 6’ tall and 187 pounds

There are cups for modesty coverage, but no support, so if you are not perky it may not work well for you. I did see someone in a picture wearing a bandeau under and it looked good. Also if you are smaller busted may you not care for the suit since there is no lifting or pushing breasts in for cleavage. I have a long torso and the suit just barely worked for me. I had to pull it high, so the front crotch coverage was skimpy and there is a seam inside the bodice that stops the cups from moving up. You can see the seam through the outside (see photos). So, if you are tall and long torso the cups may not go high enough for you. Short, average or slightly long torso should be fine.

Love it! I don't usually go for one pieces, but I am constantly losing one piece to my matching sets! Consistently leaving me with a useless, mismatched mess! So I figured a one piece would be more convenient and decided to try one out. I love this one! I was really skeptical, as someone whose weight fluctuates a lot and on top of that I bloat pretty bad too (GI issues), that this could remain comfortable. So I decided to try it on after eating a big meal and drinking some water to see how it would hold up and feel if I decided to eat a good meal at a party or if I got bloated while I was out in it and to my surprise, it felt great! It stretched with me and didn't feel too restricting or uncomfy like the one piece swimsuits from my childhood did. Cons: I wish there was a little more chest support. The pads are great for covering up better, but the support isn't really there. Just the compression of the bathing suit material. Not a huge issue for me as a size 38D, but if you had a larger chest and planned on doing anything sporty in it, it could be less comfortable. Length could be an issue for taller people. I'm 5'3 and this was just the perfect length for me, I'd imagine this length could begin to be problematic for folks over 5'7.

I’m 5’2 and about 115 lbs for reference. This bikini got me more compliments than others that cost triple. This style is reversible so it’s like two bikinis in one. It’s flattering as it’s high waisted to give you some tummy coverage and cinches in your waist. I love high waisted bikinis, but many can be too much material and look a bit frumpy. This is high cut on the leg and a thong so it’s not too much coverage, but covers where I like coverage most. Surprisingly the top gives decent support even though I’m a D cup. I tend to carry weight in my middle so I hate when material cuts into giving me a muffin top, but I just kinda stretched out the waistband with my hands before wearing and that helped the waistband become more stretchy to avoid the fat hanging over. Even my 15 year old daughter and 14 year old niece complimented me for the on trend pattern with the little red cherries, super cute! I wound up buying this pattern after I had previously ordered the black and white reversible style which is more classic than this more trendy style. This style has fast shipping and many options for different colors. I would pick your favorite color and if it fits well you may be like me and want to order in another pattern.

When I ordered this suit, it was priced at $19.99. What a steal for this cute little swimsuit. QUALITY: Surprisingly high. The top fit fine once it was on, but boy, it was a struggle to get it over my head and down my torso because it was so tight and didn't stretch much at all. Once I got everything in place, it was fine, though still a little tight around my upper torso. TUMMY CONTROL: I didn't really detect any tummy control, but the bottoms fit nicely and were high waisted. They didn't constrict at all, which I appreciate. STYLE: This is a flattering style and I'm looking forward to mixing and matching the pieces with a black bikini I already own! Very versatile pieces. NOT A MODEL: To state the obvious, I'm not a model and I didn't relish posting photos of myself in a swimsuit, but I thought it was important to see this suit on a real body. I hope my sacrifice was !

The top fit fine once it was on, but boy, it was a struggle to get it over my head and down my torso because it was so tight and didn't stretch much at all. Once I got everything in place, it was fine, though still a little tight around my upper torso. TUMMY CONTROL: I didn't really detect any tummy control, but the bottoms fit nicely and were high waisted. They didn't constrict at all, which I appreciate. STYLE: This is a flattering style and I'm looking forward to mixing and matching the pieces with a black bikini I already own! Very versatile pieces. NOT A MODEL: To state the obvious, I'm not a model and I didn't relish posting photos of myself in a swimsuit, but I thought it was important to see this suit on a real body. I hope my sacrifice was !

im going to a waterpark with my family this month & i wanted a swimsuit that was modest, bc im a mother, but not frumpy, bc im only 31 so id rather not be giving granny vibes at the waterpark. i have a big butt & hips so that is always my main concern when shopping for clothes but most especially swimsuits. i am 5'6"-5'7" & I weigh about 174 lbs & i got a size medium bc of a review i saw about sizing down. i'm really glad i got a medium instead of a large. like i mentioned before, i have a larger butt/hip area, while having much smaller chest area... the size large would have been way too large in the cheat area for me personally. this dress has a good bit of stretch & its a nice material. if you're a girlie that has a bigger chest & your main concern is heavy support in that area, this is not the swim dress for you. otherwise, I would definitely say this is one of the better experiences I have had with ordering a swimsuit online.

I tried Axesea's front zip suit and the zipper was too short to get in and out of the suit, a shame since their suits are a little more compression and have flattering ruching. (I bought a back zip one which is easy off but beastly to get on). I also have a Daci rashguard 2 piece, nice quality, but their 1 piece was too short in the torso. But this SELINK has some things going for it-- easy on and off, not huge boob pads that make you look like a 50's movie starlet. Excuse the odd poses,( and messy bathroom lol) but I was moving around seeing if the suit would stay put since I swim laps-- and it does( I also had underwear on under it, as I do for trying on suits). The aqua/black pattern is very flattering, even if the suit is not as snatched in as some other brands. I am not young, but it holds in older skin well enough! I got a medium, because other reviews said it runs large, and you want it smaller because it has a little give in the water. I am 5'9, 145 lb 36-28-37 w big ribcage and not much boobs, hips or belly. If you have a large chest or tush you might try an L, as well as M, at my height. I was really shocked this suit was long enough, I usually buy long torso suits. It does cut up a little bit on my bum, but without undies under and when it's wet I expect it will cover the cheeks. Overall very pleased with it. Can't attest to it's salt water capabilities, as bought it past my swim season, but price is good and it keeps sun of my arms and chest. Since the material is thin, I'd treat it delicately, no dragging your bum on the side of the pool, etc.

Right off the bat, I will say my girlfriend and I had high hopes for this. We loved the retro look and style of it in the pictures, so we ordered it. It came faster than almost any product we've ordered. My gf is 5'6 and 140 lbs. Not necessarily overweight but there is a small 40 year year old 'thicker in the middle' thing happening, although she hits the bike hard after work. The top was fantastic. She wears a Medium in most swimsuits, so we ordered a Medium. The top was incredibly cute and fit her 34C chest nicely. The bottom was horrible and looked like a Sumo diaper. While her tummy isn't flat, she doesn't have a big belly yet these bottoms really made her middle look wide and barrel shaped. Going up a size wouldn't be the answer ... it was a combination of the fit and her body shape. We've decided to throw out the bottoms, keep the top and pair it with a solid black standard bikini bottom. For teens and very svelte body types, it might work for you. I'd imagine XS or S sizes would look okay, especially if you have a long torso with a thin waist/stomach. For average sized girls that don't have a perfectly flat tummy, it's probably going to look strange. We ordered the black and white gingham matching top & bottom set. It looks somewhat like the pictures, except the pattern is a little lighter grey and looks printed on up close. If it fit right, it would absolutely be retro cute ... and the top really is nice when paired with a different style bottom. Overall, my recommendation is to order true to size and if you are a little pair shaped, don't attempt this. It looked so bad, my gf didn't want to post a picture of her wearing it. I'd recommend smaller body styles wear this best ... however, the suit has a fantastic retro 1940s or 1950s style which is very hard to find. I hope my honest description and pictures help you make an informed shopping decision.

I have ordered at least 10 suits on Amazon and none of them have worked until this one. I’m at 38H bust, 154lbs and 5’7”. It’s nearly impossible for me to find something that fits top and bottom that holds me in correctly, but this one checks all the boxes!! I ordered a medium and it’s perfect

I didn't expect to love this surf suit as much as I do, but it is AWESOME! I hate constantly putting on sunscreen, so it really helps with that. It's super comfortable everywhere on my body. I'm 5'2", 34DDD cup, 124 pounds, very evenly proportioned, and bought a size medium. I might have been able to buy a size small, but didn't want it to be tight fitting for athletic activities. I was worried that the built in shelf bra wouldn't support me but it does, and I'm no spring chicken. I took the removable cup pads out and go without them. I'm older so I appreciate the extra coverage on my upper arms, thighs, and tummy. I'm out there to have fun, no worry about my body.I spent 3 weeks wearing it daily on the beach hiking, kayaking, SUP, swimming, jetskiing, tubing, etc. on Lake Superior (pretty rough environment). It was super convenient for longs hikes that had swimming opportunities; I wore it with triathlon swim sneakers. So nice to go all day long with just a backpack and no other clothes! I thought it would look weird in a non-surfing environment, but it didn't; women actually asked where they could get one. I wear it all day on the dog beach in southern California as well. It's funny, when I wear it now, I think I understand why they wear those one-piece sleek space suits in sci-fi movies, they're totally comfortable and make your feel good, ha ha ha.I don't know how it would hold up in chlorinated water, but most suits don't do well in chlorine and I wear a normal swimsuit in the pool anyway. I am careful with washing it because I want it to last so I put it in a lingerie bag in the washing machine, and hang to dry. I'm most likely going to buy other versions of this same suit in the different fabrics from this same company so I have some variety. I have several other surf suits from different companies, but this one is my favorite.

34 B/36A ordered both small and medium to try. First off the color is darker than pictured. Second and more problematic; these swim tops contain some sort of UPF treatment. Since there is no information on the tags as to how the UPF treatments are achieved I did not try them on. From my research a lot of UPF clothing is chemically treated with chemicals that may not be bodysafe. The desirable way is via the tightness of a fabric's weave and there are companies that do it this way. Since Amazon has chosen not to disclose how their UPF resistance is achieved I am returning these swim tops and can't recommend them due to body safety issues. This information should be in the product description so consumers can make an informed choice before purchasing. The versions I purchased were Amazon Prime with free returns and I am returning for free via UPS drop off.

I can’t get over how amazing this batting suit feels on. I absolutely HATE bathing suits and try to hide my mommy tummy with swim dresses. This one is so flattering and comfy! All of my Hilor, Miraclesuit, and la blanca suits are size Large, XL or 12-14. I was hesitant to get a smaller size, but the size chart showed I’d fit in a medium. IT DOES!! Go by your normal shirt size. I’m 5‘0“, 147 lbs, 36D. I’m afraid a large would be too big in the chest, but the torso might look better if it had some extra fabric. It fits me great everywhere else. There’s tons of booty coverage (even with the skirt flipped up) and my boobs aren’t spilling out. Even when it’s wet, it fits me like a glove! That’s another thing: it doesn’t look see-through when it’s wet! I love the asymmetrical skirt and the little tie, but make sure to tie it tight! Mine fell off in the hot tub. The material is soft and stretchy and has a nice ribbed texture. Definitely ordering another one. I can’t decide between green, orange or black. Black will hide more, so if you’re self-conscious of your tummy like me, get black. Hope this helps ☀️

I ordered the medium. Glad I did. :) I’ve put on some pounds after my last baby. Can’t lose it like I used to now that I’m in my 40’s. I’ve been having a hard time finding a swimsuit that makes me still feel young, that’s still age appropriate AND hides some of my problem areas. This swimsuit is a winner for me! The entire swimsuit is lined. The ribbed material along with the lining helps hide outlines of your private areas… you know exactly what I’m talking about! Ha! The ruching is flattering. It helps create a nice waistline and hides some unwanted rolls and belly button indentation. It comes with removable cups. I took the cups out. There was enough ruching and material to hide my nipples. The bottoms provide good coverage. The straps are adjustable. The side cinching area is 3” long. Cinched all the way up, the swimsuit is 2” higher on the sides. The side ties help camouflage denting of the hips. Very comfortable swimsuit. I have an average sized torso. This swimsuit has plenty of stretch and material with the ruching that I feel it would work great with longer torsos too. Great quality material and stitching. This has become my new fav and I plan to order a second color for this summer.

I’m not sure what’s going on with the stitching inside the padding but it’s so unflattering, it looks like built in nipples.. there is a hole to remove the pads but it makes no difference. I don’t know why it was designed like this. Other than that I like the colour and the bottoms are as expected. It’s not cheeky it’s an actual thong so be ware of that. I’m 5ft 120lbs and ordered a M but a S would have been more form fitting.

This swimsuit top is SO flattering and very adjustable- you put it on like a front closure bra and then tie it in the front as loose or as tight as you need. The straps are adjustable like bra straps. The coverage is just right for me, shows enough to be sexy but still covers everything it needs to. The front closure straps are nice because it pulls your boobs together for cleavage. I have 520cc high profile implants but a wide set chest/torso so I have great side boob but not much cleavage unless I have something pulling the girls together. The top feels secure, reasonably supportive especially because of how adjustable it is, and good quality. Hopefully it will hold up once worn. I didn’t love the bottoms. I was expecting a little bit higher cut on the sides because of the shape but that’s more of an illusion. It doesn’t come up any higher than any standard bikini bottom so if you’re looking for a higher cut suit that you can pull up over the love handles this isn’t it. It was also a bit strange because the back of the suit was relatively loose (my glutes measure 40” around so it’s not for lack of booty) while the front looked too small. There are elastic lines in the picture from my underwear but there are no elastic lines from the suit bottom itself. The brown color is true to product picture- a sort of faded muted brown, not a rich chocolatey color like I was hoping, but still looks fine. I’m keeping this suit because the top is fabulous. I’m going to order it in more colors to match other swim suit bottoms I have. For reference I ordered a medium- I’m 5’6” 155 lbs, 40” around glutes, 29”-30” waist currently, 32” under bust and 39” over bust measurements. I would suggest ordering based on your bottom size because the top is super adjustable and the bottoms are not. Hope this is !

Ok. I'm not one to post bikini pics publicly, BUT...when I say I'm impressed with CUPSHE bikinis, I...AM...IMPRESSED! This is the first time I've bought a bikini that's not a mix-n-match when it comes to sizing and I bought 2 from CUPSHE based on other reviews with photos/measurements, etc, and I love both! So, I hope I'm able to pay it forward... I'm 5'5", 117lb, more junk in the trunk than treasure in the chest, so I normally have to buy a size larger in bottoms. I went with a medium and it was perfect! (In both that I purchased!) I feel body-confident in it, it doesn't ride up as I move, it appears well-made (no poor stitching), no snags in the ribbed outer material, and the color is beautiful. I was worried the side cutouts would create a fleshy overflow, like some bras can, but not at all! The fit was perfectly secure without being constricting. Another small bonus is how the tags are attached. I'm a tag remover and these were perfect ~ attached by a string, rather than the tag directly being attached to the bikini itself. Easily removed with no itchy scratchy factor from leftover tag bits. It arrived with all tags attached, hygienic liner in place, and in a zipper type plastic bag. I definitely recommend CUPSHE and will probably be purchasing more myself soon!

The first thing I should make clear is that I am writing this review for three different purchases: the hot pink retro high waist, and the same set in white (I purchased after receiving the pink suit). And a third white bottom from another vendor for comparison.I am 5’2”, 137lbs, 55yo, athletic build with 34D/36C chest. I have things to hide in the abdomen and was seeking this retro high waist look, and a top with more coverage.I received the hot pink suit in a medium. The bust fit, but the bottoms didn’t appear to come up as high waisted as the photos. They are more below or at the belly button (and I’m short). The leg cut is also much higher than in the images. That said, I could pull it off and needed a suit fast and kept it, even though it clearly wasn’t as pictured. The material is fine but it doesn’t really offer tummy control.I decided to get one in white thinking that perhaps it was a fluke with the bottoms on the pink suit. I ordered the same size M in white. And don’t you know this white bottom that should have been exactly the same or better was even SMALLER in the rise than the pink bottoms. Not at all what I was expecting, and it was perplexing they would be different to what is pictured.I wanted the white bottoms as pictured so I looked at other places. Found a pair, the exact looking bottoms and ordered a pair. They ARE exactly like the picture on this page, but I didn’t get them here.So if you are looking for the true high waist retro bottoms (50’s) which are extremely flattering, it’s not what you will get with this page. The bottoms sent do not match what is pictured. However, it’s a good suit if the waist height doesn’t matter so much to you.My photos: the side leg difference between the pink and white bottoms in the exact same size, and the side leg/waist difference between the white bottoms sent by this company vs the white bottoms with the black tag I purchased elsewhere.

The BERLOOK one piece was a nice surprise. I did not realize form the picture it was actually velvet, which turned out to be soft and stretchy, and beautiful color. It looks equally nice before and after getting into the water. I took it on vacation, wore it a lot, especially for the evening swims or during a bit colder day. It looked very elegant and felt comfortable. Fits to size, for reference I am size 6-8 (Medium), 34DD in the bust, and short torso. The reason I took a star off is the lining. It is white, peeks put every time you turn. It is also adding the bulk to already thicker than usual and completely non-transparent fabric, and it has different elasticity too. What I did? I removed the lining and sewed the edges by hand. I am happy with the final outcome and wear the suit a lot. I am sure most people would not go that far. The straps are very long, but I do not see it as a problem rather an increase of options. My favorite look is crisscrossing on the back, then tying around the waist. I tried a few different versions. If you figured your favorite option – just cut the straps shorter, it won’t have any impact on the suit. I recommend this swimming suit, it is pretty and comfortable, looks dressy with a sarong.

Once it was wet it still held up and didn’t fit weird. The only thing I would mention is while holding my toddler the front would want to roll down a little since there isn’t a hard seam line around the edging. Aside from that would highly recommend. I’m 5’7” and 128lbs for reference and ordered the medium.

My only con is I wish there was a tiny bit more coverage to where it would actually cup the entire butt but I'll still confidently wear them in public. The colors on this suit are vibrant and fun, and a bright color like this complements [my] very pale skin quite well. The two-tone color combo gives two-piece vibes, but has the comfort and security of a one piece, which is exactly what I want while chasing my small children around a pool/beach and reminiscing about the summers I spent actually relaxing in a hot pink two piece ten years ago. In terms of appearance, this suit accentuates the chest, but comfortably holds everything in place, and the cutout shows off the best part of my abs- a well-developed two-pack, and downplays the post-childbirth-work-in-progress lower abs/stomach with some well-positioned ruching. I would give this 20 stars if I could, and I am contemplating buying a second one if, god forbid, something happens to mine and it is no longer available. It’s THAT good- so good I am including a picture of myself in said suit. If that’s not confidence, I don’t know what is. Don’t let the camera angles and natural light fool you; I’m 5’ 3” and 135-140 lbs.

I've never reviewed a swimsuit before but am doing so now because, at least out of the package, I love this one. This piece is definitely more comfortable than the other 3, slightly "sexier" styles I own. The straps are adjustable and wide enough to not cut in to the shoulders. The bust is comfortable on my 32G chest, with enough lift to not smoosh me in, sides high enough that I'm not spilling out, and an overall construction that holds "the girls" front and centre with a natural but not scandalous degree of cleavage. The sewn-in cups do not show. I don't notice any tummy control as advertised (hence the 4-star rating), but the shirring is flattering and brings out the waist-hips curves. The leg openings are low enough to cover my butt and stay put, without cutting in to the skin. I haven't worn this in the water yet but with the dark colors I'm not worried about see-through. Bonus: no horrible chemical smell like the other suits I've ordered online. For reference, I am 5'6", 135 lbs. 41-31-40, usually size 6/M. From the underbust seam to the leg opening on the side this suit measures 11" in the size M. I am high / shortwaisted but, not having a waistband, the torso also fits comfortable and looks nice. May order in another colorway... Edit: I have now worn this in the water; no transparency or stretching issues. Although this suit covers more skin than most of my others, it still got a "hubba hubba" out of the boyfriend :)

I never leave reviews but this suit is so amazing I just had to. I’m a 71 year, old 5’4”, 131 pound grandmother with a long torso. I wanted a flattering suit for my back yard pool and for our upcoming trip to the beach. I purchased size medium / 6-8. Having a long torso is a problem with one piece suits. If I don’t get a long torso suit, it pulls on my shoulders and my “cheeks” show. I read the reviews and because both short and tall women were raving about this suit I thought I’d give it a try. It arrived quickly and on time. I put it on right away and think it fits and looks great! Not going for sexy at my age-just fit, modest, comfortable, and flattering. I was looking at another company whose prices were exorbitant because after all swimsuits wear out very fast because of chlorine exposure so to have to pay a lot of money for a suit is a waste. The bra is wonderful. I need a lot of support and this suit delivers. The color is what it looked like on the Amazon app and it’s very pretty. I haven’t gone in the pool yet but will very soon. I’m just so excited I had to say how happy I am with this suit! One slight negative is the boy short bottom is slightly loose in the back and a little long in the front but the skirt is long enough to cover that little faux pas!

I am the type of person who would never dare post a picture/review for bathing suits, but after receiving these I had to write one because WOW. I know how hard it is to do bathing suit shopping online and pictures help a lot but I feel like my body type is the least I can find reviews about. I had a hard time deciding what size to buy so I ordered 2 pairs in a size small and medium. I am small chested up top, small on the bottom, no hips, no waist, and I have long legs for being short. For guidance I am 5’2 , 126 Lbs, 34A bra size, 31inches on my waist , and 38 inches on my hip. As you can see my body type just varies on the given size chart . I can be a size 4-6, 6-8, small, or medium. It’s hard for me. For bathing suits I normally need a medium bottom, and small top . Surprisingly the size small was way too tight up top, so I tried the medium set and it fit PERFECT! It’s a little tight up top still, but I prefer it that way so I don’t have to worry about my top falling off . I gained a lot of weight and have been working out to lose it . My fat normally just goes to the back of my thighs and my sides giving me love handles. The bathing suit bottom kind of acts like a tummy tuck for my love handles lol. Also, the bottoms are a little cheeky and for someone like me who is small on the bottom, it still works! I still have cellulite that shows, but this bathing suit makes me not care about that. I feel good and comfortable ! I finally found a good bathing suit and I’m going to order so many more of these in different colors! P.S Both bathing suits in the pictures are the size Medium(:

I am 5'7", 145lbs, bust:35" waist: 30" hips: 39" I wear a 32D bra and typically wear a size 4. Ordered a size Medium. I recieved the suit and was so excited! The colors are beautiful, in person, and the suit seems well made. It fits pretty good, but I am still a little unsure about the waist area. I can't decide if I am just insecure about my tummy or of it would be better for the seam to hit in a slightly different area. I do wish the tie was a little longer so that I could tie it into a bow without pulling it so tight, but that isn't a big deal. The zipper is good quality. I do need help zipping it up, but I can unzip it without assistance. (I forgot to zip in the pic...oops!) The coverage on the bottom is decent and I don't think it will ride up in the front or back. The "bikini line" is a perfect cut and the elastic doesn't cut in, but it also won't flap open with a breeze. I don't have particularly perky boobs, and there is enough support and coverage on the top for me. There is also a lining in the top with a seam that can be seen through the material (see pic) but it doesn't bother me. The suit is definitely opaque and I do not worry about it being see-through. The swimsuit is very comfortable and great for watersports, chasing the kids, etc while still looking stylish. I can see myself wearing it all day without any issues.

I liked the quality for the price. I wanted a swim top that wouldn’t leave strap tan-lines so the removable straps are nice. I’m 5’8, 155lbs, 38C cup, medium build with average length torso and a medium seems the right fit, not too tight nor too loose. The strap claps seem durable and the clasp areas are sewn well. The criticisms I have are that the “ladies” didn’t have any lift which I prefer at least a little bit of in a suit. And the cup liners are sewn in so I cannot add/put in cup liners to help boost. But some people may prefer the liners to be sewn in. With the straps on and tightened you may get a little lift but not much and I bought for the purpose of not having the straps on. I have not worn it in a pool yet so can’t speak about that but may come back to update. I’m keeping and may try to find cups I can just place between the inside of the suit and me because it seems secure enough on me that the cups wouldn’t fall out. It is very flattering through the torso though and love the white. Probably would only wear to the pool and not a beach or lake though as I could foresee it turning off-white/getting stained fairly quickly based on the material.

I tried this on today, I WISH I had taken a photo. To not have an underwire, this top is incredible. I am a 34C, but was previously a 38DD. After going from 228lbs to 134lbs over the last few years, and having and breastfeeding two kids, the girls don’t exactly stand up anymore. With many tops, these things fall down like a broken miniblind lol. You would NEVER know it while I’m wearing this top. The fit is so flattering. It offers full coverage AND support with no underwire! The straps are easy to adjust, and also very flattering. I can’t attest to how much support would be offered to my much larger chested ladies, as you can’t really adjust the back much, and the shoulders only go so far. But when I tell you this thing is comfortable, my goodness. This top checks all my boxes of what I look for in a top: no underarm flab, support, no boobs falling out, and extreme comfort. I’m on my way to buy another in a different color right now! I will try to add a photo in this review later today! Edit to add: it is quite the challenge taking a photo by yourself. Dont question it, just buy the top. I’ve already ordered 2 other colors.

Sizing: After looking at the picture of this swimsuit on the model I HAD to get it. I read the reviews on sizing and they are very conflicting. I ignored the advice to size up and am so glad I did. The swimsuit fit perfectly with a little wiggle room in the bottom. The top was a tad snug, but I wanted it that way so that it doesn’t slip down while I’m walking. My weight fluctuates quite a bit so I will put my current measurements down as well as my heavier measurements (it has fit me through all of these sizes). Measurements: Height: 5’5” Weight: 125-135lbs Waist: 26-28in (smallest part of waist) Hips: 36-38.5in Chest: 32C Build: Athletic with weight mostly in thighs Fit: The bottoms are not as high waisted as advertised which I’m not a huge fan of, but I do like the front coverage they provide. The back is cheeky, but I actually felt comfortable with it because of how full coverage the front is. (If you want full coverage bottoms, front and back, these are not the ones lol). I think they run true to size IF your bra size is on the size chart. (Just the bra size, the rest of the chart is inaccurate so I would take it as a mild suggestion). If you are very chesty or like to wear your clothes tighter, size up. Material: The material is excellent quality. Thick enough to feel secure, but soft/absorbent enough for the water. I rated the swimsuit 4 stars because my first swimsuit from this seller came with a wire sticking up out of the side. It was sharp and poked me when I was wearing it. Amazon fixed this super quickly (2 days) and sent me another one that did not have that issue. Therefore, I don’t know if I had a fluke or if the quality control is lacking. Regardless, I would buy the swimsuit again.

Loooove this top and the comfort it provided. Highly recommend! I ordered a Small and am 120lbs, 5'2", 32B. The fabric was nice and soft and stretchy, and I loved that it went down my torso a little further than some (if the knot was looser). Nice that it's adjustable in that way and I could tighten the knot, or completely tuck it under for a different look. The buttons are functional, which is also cool. The cups slid around a bit and needed to be adjusted throughout the day. Might not be an issue for bigger chests!

I only write reviews if the product was horrible, or I feel like people need to know how awesome the product really is. I’m an avid Amazon customer & rely heavily on authentic reviews. I also am obsessed with bathing suits, so I’ve purchased very expensive ones and very inexpensive ones (such as this one!). I’ve tried other bathing suit sellers on Amazon due to the amazing prices, but this is the first bathing suit I can honestly say I love. In the past, the Amazon bikinis I’ve purchased I wore only once, or absolutely hated the fabric & make of the suit. For example, I’ll never buy a Zaful suit again. 😳 I noticed that this suit had various reviews from all types of body shapes & most say that it is a great buy. I 100% agree that all women should give this suit a try! + Positives and - Negatives: + I rated this bikini a 4/5 only because I am comparing it to all bikinis I’ve ever purchased. Therefore, for the actual price & quality of the suit, I’d say it’s 5/5. + There is NO filter on my photo only REALLY good natural lighting. This photo shows an honest depiction of the color. I think it is very flattering. I have a medium complexion. + Fits and snugs in all the right places while also making me feel comfortable enough to run around on a beach, however.... - I am right on the edge of 5’6” and 130ish lbs, so I decided to buy a medium because I tend to squeeze into my bottoms when I buy a small with Amazon. Due to this, my bottom is a little baggy in the back, and may ride up at times but it isn’t a big deal to me. I’ve had worse. + Right after I got this suit, I purchased a small in the red. I’ll update this if I think the small is too small for me! Gist: This suit is flattering on many body types. Get it!

Perfect swimsuit for anyone who wants a smidge more modesty but still wants to look cute! After having a baby and weight loss in my mid-30’s I was looking for something that would cover a little bit more but didn’t look matronly and this suit checks all the boxes. I feel like I can comfortably play in the water and sand with my toddler without any cheeks or peeks faux pas. I’m pretty petite at 4’10” 110lbs and purchased a size small in army green. I took my photos in midday sunlight without any indoor lights on and feel like the color is accurate. With the adjustable straps fully tightened, I feel completely secure up top (I’m a 34C bra size). The skirt hits at the perfect length, something I was worried about being too long since I’m so short, and the skirt being built in will be great so there won’t be any fussing to readjust it with movement and getting wet. I honestly didn’t have high expectations for a random swimsuit from Amazon but I can confidently say that I’m extremely impressed. The structure and seams feel well constructed so I have high hopes this swimsuit will hold up for a long while. I washed it and hung to dry after taking these photos and it held up fine. I’ll update my review after a few wears and putting it to the test but I suspect that won’t be needed. 10/10. Do it, buy the swimsuit.

So I actually ordered a S first, but it was just too clingy for me personally, and I reordered the top in M. Now, I absolutely love this swim top! The fabric is silky smooth, and stitched well. I paired my top with swim leggings for modesty reasons, and feel so comfortable! Size - I’m 5’4” 130 lbs, and while the S did fit, I just didn’t like how tight it was. The M is definitely more roomy - like your favorite comfy T-shirt. With the draw strings pulled all the way, the length falls around my hips. I’m not sure yet how well it’ll hold up to washes. Overall though, this is my new favorite swim top, and I can’t wait to hit the beach! Give it a try!

I am 5’8” and 135 pounds for reference. Bra size 32C (or 34B in some brands). I’m 42 years old and I’ve had three c-sections—with a poochy lower belly scar to show for them now. Suits with tummy control and higher waisted bottoms are key. This suit has both of those, looks good and covers all the key spots without looking either too frumpy or too revealing. Score. I initially got this suit in red in size medium, and I still have that one and wear it on occasion. It works fine actually. The small is just a better fit for my shape. The medium is a little large in the top (particularly in the shoulders, and upper boob area) and has a little too much extra material in the front ruching on the bottoms. Still cute and perfectly wearable at the pool with the kids and whatnot. Just not as flattering as the small in my case. I’d love something that could magically flatten my post baby x 3 belly completely, but this one does a good enough job for me. It controls and hides the c-section scar area very well. And the fluttery top adds nice emphasis up there, and draws the eyes away from any hint of poochy business below, so that’s a plus for sure. It’s a great suit overall. Sexy without being over the top in any way. Comfortable too. Yay. Pics of size small black suit in first three images. Last is pic of the red in medium, mainly showing the extra material in the top.

I ordered a size small which I'd say was an accidentally ideal fit for my 5'5 130 lb self. The top seems to run smaller than the bottom. I very much liked the length of the top; I think I'm "long torso" and this was perfect for that. If you typically find that tankini tops are prone to being shorter than you like, this one works well and as I tried it on (in my room, because it's January in the midAtlantic here) it never "rode up." The bottom skirt is longer than some I own and offers plenty of coverage....I love the modest look but I think it's almost too long for me. Or maybe it was just that the skirt is more "full" and kinda more "flouncy" than I was expecting. Both the top and the bottom have a lot of coverage and overlap. and the waist of the skirt was a fine fit for me (usually I wear a 6 bottoms these days...with a few brands I can squeeze into a 4, for perspective). The star deduction is for an oddity that may or may not be a one-off; The top and bottom seem to be just ever so slightly different fabric "sheen" levels....like a slightly different fabric all together. The top I loved, it's a thick black matte fabric that looks higher end and is of very good quality. The bottom, however, is my star deduction as the fabric seemed different, more thin and a bit more shiny/sheen than the top. I didn't like the bottom as well as the top, but I don't know how noticeable it would be to any onlookers, in the water, or even to other buyers, but I was looking carefully at the item for the purposes of the review and might be heavy on the scrutiny here. Nice suit overall, and the nice thing about tankinis of course is that the pieces can be mixed and matched, so I'll probably do that as I prefer the top but others might feel the opposite! I think bathing suits are just tough all around so 4 stars here is a win :-]

I ordered a variation of sizes/colors of this bathing suit to compare them. I ordered a small blue and a medium orange from this seller to see which size/color I liked better. I love this beautiful blue one piece swimsuit. The color is fabulous. The material is on the heavier side but not too thick. It’s not see through. It is a little trying to get out of when wet, the zipper opening is short, which could be an issue. The built in shorts have a 3” inseam, the skirt is built into the suit and covers the built in shorts. The sleeves have a cuff like end so they stay down and there are thumb holes. The sleeves are tighter fitting and really can’t be rolled up. I did roll them once over the thumb hole so they didn’t look long, but you can’t push them up over your elbow. There is a built in bra and the pads are removable. I removed them because they were located too high for me:0 There is plenty of room in the small size for a bigger chest by removing the pads. I am 5’5”, 118lbs, 34B wearing a small. It fits well but still has bust room with the pads inserted. I have a long torso so I feel the built in bra is a little high but removing the pads helped. This suit offers great sun protection but I do wish it was offered in short sleeves. I get a little too hot in these long sleeves if I’m not in the water. Once in the water or after you get out of the water it’s very cooling. There are two pockets on the outside. Overall, I think it fits me well, and it’s stylish. I feel the busy blue leaf color combo pattern would be forgiving to most figures and recommend if you are looking for a one piece long sleeve swimsuit. It’s really stunning and I get so many compliments when I wear it.

This basically looks like a sports bra, nothing really special, a very basic style. However, it's pretty well done for what it is. The fit is nice - I'm 5', 115lbs, normally a 32/34A and the small fits as expected. I like that the top is cut a little higher and everything is adjustable. I was able to get a comfortable yet snug fit so that I don't have to worry about anything hanging out when I'm with my kids (while I'm bending, lifting, etc). The seams all seem sturdy, the hardware is all metal, and there are small pieces of boning on either side of the bra part so it won't bunch up. I like having some padding, so I like that there are pockets for removeable cups, but the included ones are the cheap thin ones that fold and bunch. I've bought higher quality cups for other bathing suits and bralettes and it makes a world of difference, so will probably do that here. I removed the cups and washed the top in the regular laundry and it came out perfect. Overall, a good top, but I'd recommend looking for a discount.

Top: the top fits well for me, and I appreciate it as decent coverage as it made it much more comfortable to be active in it. The only complaints I have is that due to being halter top. It can sag down on the string more than I like (pictured) which means it has to be adjusted by pulling it wider, but I won’t blame the suit for that one. The padding in it is typical, and I didn’t have any problems with getting nippy. I liked that it was backless and the halter strings were long, which was ideal Bottoms: these bottoms are really comfortable, however, the problems that I have with them is that the lining of the inside of suit is white, and I bought it in the orange color, so the shorts roll up and look something like white underwear poking out (pictured). I really wish it was the same material, it would have gotten 5 stars with that fixed. Overall, I bought this suit specifically for the bottoms, because I did not want to maintain a bikini line and even with the shorts rolling up I was pretty well covered. Ties: the ties on the suit make it really adjustable which I appreciate. Each tie is functional, not just decorative and so it makes more customizable to what fit you like. I really like the ones on the bottoms, really helps my butt look less flat, which is an issue with higher waisted bottoms. The ties make the suit more finicky overall, especially the first couple wears but once they’re tight they will stay for the most part, even while active. Fit: I am a 5’9” person with wide shoulders and wear a 34c bra, and I sometimes wear size small tops but am always safe sizing up to a medium. Typically I am a dress size 6 but occasionally a 4 and size 6 jeans and wear medium size underwear. I bought a size small and this fit me very well, definitely glad I bought the size recommended to me, it was very accurate.

My preteen daughter is 5'0" and 100# with a developing body. So, she wanted a modest swimsuit with a built-in bra and lined cups, which are not available in kid-sized swimsuits. So, I got this swimsuit in a small--which fits a little long in length but fits in the body very well. It is easy to take up the straps a little with some simple sewing to accommodate her height. The built-in cups are not too shaped, so she's not sticking out in places she doesn't have, but it provides the coverage we were looking for. The colors are great for a young girl, and the style is surprisingly fun. She actually was smiling ear to ear trying it on. The style will also allow her to play at the water park without worrying about any malfunctions. The fabric is thick, less stretchy than her other swimsuits. The straps are not adjustable, so that may be an issue for some. Easy to put on, just step in and pull up. I highly recommend this swimsuit.

I got this because I wanted a swimsuit that covered my middle for SUP boarding (one less place to apply sunscreen), I love purple, and this was the first one-piece swimsuit I came across that looked cute AND had shorts!! For a few reasons, I only wear shorts swimsuit bottoms, so when I saw this one had that under the skirt, I was sold. It looks adorable IMO (and I've gotten compliments on it). I was glad to find the seam on the shorts is just one horizontal seam further back that isn't very noticeable--I've had some shorts bottoms with bulky, irritating seams in obnoxious places. I wasn't quite sure if I'd need a small or medium, but when I ordered, the purple was only in a small, so I bought that and hoped for the best. I'm 5'8.5" with a long torso, about 122 lbs, and wear a 34D bra. The small is almost perfect--so I suspect the medium would have been a little too big. The top edge of the bust is the tiniest bit tight, but not enough to bother me while paddling on a SUP board or cause any chafing. The only thing is I had to adjust the straps to the the longest they could go, and the shorts still try to ride up my crack. However, I've been stretching the straps gently which is helping, and when the bottom is wet (I haven't gotten the entire suit wet yet), it does weigh down/stretch the suit just a tiny bit which helps it not ride up. However, if I were any taller (or possibly, the same height but slightly thicker), the small wouldn't have worked.

I found these bottoms to be comfortable and well-made, especially considering the low price point. But I do think they run a little small. I'm 5'8" and 118 pounds at the moment, with a 26" waist and 36" hips, placing me on the smaller end of a size small according to the sizing chart. The size small feels a bit snug over my hips and creates a little muffin top where there wasn't one before, especially from the back (see photo). My weight fluctuates and I don't think these will be comfortable anymore when I inevitably gain 5-10 pounds. For now they're fine. I think these bottoms would fit best on women who, unlike me, do not have a long torso and/or aren't over 5'7". I know they're intended to be low-rise but if they were any lower I'd be showing crack. And "full back coverage" is a relative concept. These cover more of my butt than many other options but they cannot fully contain my cheeks-- and I have minimal booty at the moment! I ordered a few bikini bottoms off Amazon and the ones that look best on me are the ones that are straight-up about being "cheeky". I think for many women these will be ~accidentally cheeky~ and that's not often the most flattering option (but go on girl, it's yo world). All that said, I'm definitely going to wear these to swim and body surf in the ocean this summer. Big waves will not budge these bottoms and that's worth a lot. Also, the fabric is very nice-- opaque, stretchy, soft, solid stitching, and fully lined everywhere. In the pics I'm wearing these bottoms as a set with the "Smart & Sexy Women's Swim Secret Mega Push-up Halter Bikini Top" which I love and recommend enthusiastically.

I was not expecting this bathingsuit to be soooo flattering. I have a larger chest and finding any kind of suit with support that looks flattering is a challenge. The tummy control ACTUALLY WORKS too unlike 99% of bathingsuits that claim to have "tummy control", but in reality just have extra fabric to appear that way, but in reality, does nothing to suck your stomach in. It's not AMAZING with this suit...but it's better than most. I'm not a fan of the double straps though. It's a little weird looking and getting tan lines from it gives me anxiety. Plus you can see some of the white lining exposed right above the cups (you can see in my photos), which doesn't look good...but the fit of the cleavage outweighs all of that and I went ahead and ordered this suit in multiple colors. I feel great in it and it's super comfortable. I'm 5'7 about 190lbs and about a 36-38F cup. An XL fit me good. Slightly on the bigger side, but not enough where I could have sized down to a Large. I'm 120lbs and 5'2, with a bit of a booty and a large B cup.As with all bathing suits, you run the risk of the bottom and top not being a perfect fit. The top fit great, but the bottom indented into my sides unless I hiked them up higher... which was basically giving my lady bits an uncomfortable wedgie. During said wedgie phase, the suit fit beautifully.. Like, so good - but it was so uncomfortable. I opted to return vs. exchange for a bigger size, because I knew a bigger sized top would have been too big... even if the bottoms fit great. So, if this fits your shape perfect top and bottom, you will have an INCREDIBLY cute suit. Although, if it doesn't - you might just be sad like me haha.Worth a try if you have time to return and your life doesn't depend on having it.

I am torn in my review of this bathing suit between 3 and 4 stars, 3.5 stars is more appropriate. I think this tankini will fit those with a fuller figure the best. I am 5’5”, 118 lbs, 34B wearing a small. It fits me very loose so I need more neck strap support. The neck strap is very stretchy and hard to tie tight. Here are my pros and cons.Pro’s: I really love love the vivid pink color (read my cons on the color) and the front look of the tankini. The front design is visually very attractive and hides imperfections:) The front of the whole bathing suit is lined. The twisted boob area is flattering. The bra is sewn in and the cups are not flimsy inserts you can remove. The fabric is light, thin and soft.Con’s: The straps that tie around the neck are too stretchy and loosens quickly. Adjustable shoulder to the back straps would have made the top more secure. The tag mentions this garments distinctive looked is derived from a special fluorescent die, which is prone to fading. It will be a shame if this top fades drastically :( I only suggest quickly handwashing, adding vinegar to the water (do not let it sit and soak) and not wearing any light colored clothing over this tankini, especially if the tankini is damp or wet, it could bleed onto other clothing!!!! It’s advised not to hang this garment until it’s dry?? Be aware this is a high maintenance very pretty swim top. I will update on the fading issue with continued use.UPDATE: hand washed in cold water with a mild soap and added a little vinegar to the tib of water. Soaked a few minutes then rinsed. There was minimal to no color bleeding or fading. Line dried.

The Charmo Frill Trim Ribbed One Piece Swimsuit with Ruffled Ruched tummy in the Navy color is Fabulous!!! I ordered the Small size and the fit is great on me. As a 45 y.o. mom, I needed something a bit less "cheeky" and these bottoms delivered a great fit, stayed put even at the beach in some fierce waves. Overall great quality for a great price! Had to post a review since I went into something of a rabbit hole perusing literally hundreds to inform my choice - thanks to all the brave ladies for putting your photos & size info on Amazon. Joining you here! I ordered both a SMALL and a MEDIUM to try, I'm 5'6" 135 pounds. When I did my measurements per the size chart, Medium seemed to be the choice (I measured my hips at 32", waist maybe 26/27") but I threw a Small in my cart too since that's what I usually wear. Ended up returning the Medium, Small it is! (Small pictured here.) All seams lay flat and cute side detailing better quality than expected for the price! These even stayed on at the beach! Super surprised since this is the first time in years I've gotten bottoms for less than $40-60 (I usually buy from Athleta, Prana or similar), I'm super active in the water & can't mess around with low quality. Wanted red, white or blue for a 4th of July party & knew these would be delivered more quickly than the catalogs. Glad I took a chance on these! Deducted one star for "flattering" only because of the slight U-shape they make below my belly, you can see that in the straight-on photo. Maybe that's how they're supposed to be styled but I find myself trying to pull up & over so the top lays straight across.

Finally a bikini bottom that does not expose half or more of my butt. I do have biniki bottoms that are skimpy but I don't always have a nice butt day if that makes sense. That's where this nice bikini bottom comes in. This bottom is not high waisted which is appreciated. I like many high waisted bottoms like shorts, leggings, but I'm not convinced high waisted bikini bottoms look good on me. This full coverage bottom is comfortably snug. The little ruch and bows on the sides add to the fun and make this bottom not too plain. The inside of this bottom is not white which I'm really glad. I find white insides a bit awkward and easily stained. This bottom will also be my go to when I go to the pool or beach to be a bit more active. There are times when I play sporty activities in the water and I want something on my butt that will stay on, that won't slide left and right, that I don't have to worry about often. Overall, a good and reliable biniki bottom. I'm 5"3, 28 waist, 36 hips, 122 lbs, and got a small.

I was looking for a modest swimsuit to wear for church camps as a youth leader and I came across this beautiful swim dress! I really liked the pattern, shape of the neck line and the cute tie up detailing! The one thing I wish it had was a longer torso with the cup pads a bit higher. It felt like I was fighting to get a bit of coverage up top and lift. The skirt portion also has an interesting sewn seam. Nothing super out there, but I did find it a bit strange and with the color of the suit, the white thread did stand out a bit. Overall, the suit was well made and comfortable. I got lots of compliments from the other young gals at my church! I didn’t see women in there 20’s without kids posting reviews, so here I am, helping a sister out! I got a size Small, I’m about 127-132lbs, athletic build, and a 32D. Hope this helps someone else!

I was really excited for this swimsuit to come in, I fell in love with the design and the pictures on the model made it look like my dream swimsuit, however, I was quite disappointed with the fit when I tried it on. The other reviews were absolutely correct about it feeling like you have to tug and pull on this swimsuit to get it to be in the correct places. The swimsuit is just so clingy and has absolutely no tummy control and, if anything, it reveals your belly even more. The built in bra was also disappointing. It was padded, but not very much and the way the built in bra part sat on the chest made me look super flat chested and it just did not look flattering, so I actually wore another padded swimsuit top I had underneath it for support. My next big complaint was the area where it transitioned into a skirt. It felt like it was placed too low, which just made it look and feel awkward. I think the placement of that area is what makes it not fit like it should and what makes it look unflattering. I ended up feeling so insecure that I just threw some beach pants over it (last pic). I ended up keeping the swimsuit (mostly because I'm too lazy to return it 🤪) and I'm hoping that maybe I will feel differently about it next time I wear it. I am 5'3 and about 127 lbs for reference and I ordered a small.

Wow, I am so surprised by how much I love this swimsuit. I'm 5'4", 130lbs, small bust, and the size small fit snugly but comfortably. I normally don't like one-piece suits because I have scoliosis, and one-pieces over-accentuate my misaligned hips. However, the structure and style of this suit mask it while also really flattering my figure. It even seems to slim down my waist and postpartum tummy. I love the straight neckline, and the built-in bra provides good support without mashing down my boobs. The thicker straps and the strings in the back are other nice details that add to this swimsuit's femininity and style. And the material is thick and feels nice, not cheap. And the turquoise color is, ugh, to die for!! It's very true to the color you see in the listing photos and is so attractive on my pale skin! I haven't worn it in water yet, but overall this suit has impressed me, and I can't wait to try it out this summer.

I am wearing a size small in the picture and I am 5’3, 135 lbs. My measurements are 36D-27-36 and I had no issues with the fit. The top was easy to put on. I purchased this bathing suit for my upcoming 50th Birthday Girls getaway trip to Hawaii. I wanted a suit that was fun and flattering. Not an easy ask, but this suit delivered. I’m 5’4”, a size 4 in most clothes, long waisted, 32DD and generally “fit”, but had a c-section 15 years ago that my stomach never recovered from. As a result, bathing suit shopping can be very frustrating since so much of it is about the tummy. This suit conceals my tummy issues (without making my skin crunch) while simultaneously giving a body lengthening affect. Also, the hip straps lengthen the look of legs by revealing skin all the way up. I am slightly long waisted, so II had to tug a bit to get the suit top closer to my neck, but it ultimately worked. Finally, the cut of the back of the suit has a modem looking cheeky effect without actually being cheeky. I hope you find this review . Good luck suit shopping and please remember to be extra kind to yourself when trying on suits.

i’m 5’4” and weigh 105lbs - bought size small. as a mom to three young kiddos, i’ve got some wrinkled skin below my belly button (and a small hernia which you can see in the pictures) from getting huge and pregnant, to now being slender and fit, so i wanted to find a bikini that wasn’t up to my armpits, but one that covered up my mommy tummmy. the top is darling, great fit. i’m 32DD and even in the size small it fits me very well. the bottoms, not so much. i had a loose extra inch on both sizes.. not to mention the crotch was saggy by about two inches. luckily i knew how to sew, so i made some quick alterations. overall: happy with the fit (now) and the color & style. very unique and darling. if i didn’t know how to sew i would have had to send it back, hence the 4 star rating.

My dog chewed the crotch out of this summers bathing suit so I needed one before the weekend and didn’t have time to go shopping so I searched amazon since I knew I could get one in at least a day. I chose this bathing suit because I’m a mom of two and I like one piece suits typically because I’ve never tried on a high wasted bikini bottom that I liked. One pieces can be a pain though especially when you have to go to the bathroom so I figured I’ll try this based on the positive reviews. Worst case scenario I could return it. And I really thought there was a good chance I would. Especially after running to kohl’s on my lunch break to try on 30 swim pieces and I didn’t like any of them. So...when I got home and tried this suit on I was pleasantly surprised. First of all I LOVE the bottoms and will use them with the top it came with but it will also look good with my other tops. The bottom is flattering and not tight or loose (well the butt is slightly loose but I’m ok with it because I mean it when I say slightly. Better than tight and have to deal with muffin top or whatever). I like the ruching of the fabric. The bottom is super cute. The top isn’t my typical style but together this suit is super cute. I really like it. The top seems to have a lot of room but fits at the same time. If I could have an extra small in the top I think I would like it more but the small is totally wearable and I’m looking forward to wearing this out. It’s cute and comfortable and I’m pleasantly surprised. For a size reference I’m 5’5 and 110 pounds.

But I won't be ordering a replacement due to the color being off.

The first swim dress I received had a major quality control issue. A portion of the skirt looked as if it had been hastily cut with a pair of scissors. Thankfully, the seller sent a replacement right away that did not have any fabric missing.I am 5’4” and slightly pear shaped. My measurements fall within a size small for every category of the size chart. Something that doesn’t always happen for me. That said, I could hear threads snapping as I barely squeezed my 37.5” hips into each swim dress and yet no visible change was noticeable. The dress looks good on and I am glad I went with print instead of a solid given the sheerness of the skirt. I have a long torso and felt like I’d maxed out the length of the torso, so this may not be enough length for a truly tall person. The straps are adjustable and while i could have lengthened the straps more, this would have resulted in the chest not being covered at the correct height. The raw hem of the skirt does make me a bit nervous about longevity, but since I plan to wear this more for occasionally building sand castles on the beach, then regular swimming in a pool, it should be able to get me through a summer or two.Overall: the look is good, the seller is responsive if there is an issue, and it’s probably a better fit if your hips are smaller than what is listed and your torso is on the somewhat short to medium end of the spectrum.

I tried different positions like moving and bending forward to see if the top would stay in place especially the bust line, and it did work perfectly. I also found this tankini top to be made well. The design is classy and elegant. The tag on the back says it’s designed in Paris. The little golden brand name plaque on the back of this tankini gives the item an elegant touch…

Fairly true to size but runs slightly small. I’m an XS/S bottom 36-37” hip and an athletic 32C bust and the S fits perfectly. Note that not all of the back straps adjust, only the bottom most strap connecting one side to the other is adjustable. The x-pattern on the straps are fixed, but where they intersect slides through the other straps, so there is a little adjusting that can happen. However, this fits like a dream! The bottoms NEVER moved while boogie boarding for an hour and a half in the Florida surf, and I never once had to dig them out of my crack. These are low rise and cheeky, which is exactly what I wanted. The S top shows off enough but also doesn’t budge, so I don’t feel like I’m ever going to stand up out of the water showing off a nipple. This is my second summer with this suit and it still looks brand new. Very well made. The bottoms are a hot-pink/bright cherry but not neon. It’s a really pretty color and the colors in the top match it perfectly. I love that every seam is an interior rolled elastic edge so there’s no pinching. Buying a second one before it’s no longer available!
